title: Echinopsis pectinata
intro: Echinopsis pectinata, commonly known as the "Old Lady Cactus," is a species of cactus in the Echinopsis genus, characterized by its unique, pectinate (comb-like) spines and vibrant, funnel-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, wounds, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Echinopsis pectinata include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Echinopsis pectinata is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments, although its use and efficacy may vary depending on the region and preparation method.
taxonomy: Echinopsis pectinata is classified within the family Cactaceae, which includes a vast array of cacti species. This classification is based on its physical characteristics, such as its columnar stem and areoles. The genus Echinopsis is a subgenus of the larger genus Cereus, which includes many species of cacti. Echinopsis pectinata's specific classification is further broken down into the subfamily Cactoideae, making it a part of the Cactaceae family's most diverse group. Within its genus, Echinopsis pectinata is often distinguished by its unique, pectinate (comb-like) spines.
